sections.paragraph: a snail, especially one without a shell, is called from its moisture and sliminess (like the Gr. λείμαξ from λείβω), from the conj. Shaph. of the verb בָּלַל No. 1, Psa 58:9, it is said of the wicked, “let them melt away … כְּמֹו שַׁבְלוּל תֶּמֶס יַהֲלֹךְ as a snail, which melts as it goes,” i.e. which emits slime, moistening its way; so that the longer it goes the more is it dissolved, and at length wastes away and dies.
